items.title: Build artifact caching for staging
source_titles: New loop process — Build artifact caching for staging: The 1m 51s Next.js build now dominates the job. Caching the .next/cache directory between runs (e.g. via GitHub Actions cache) could reduce repeat build times. Trigger observation: Next.js build time unchanged between Run 2 and Run 3 despite identical code, suggesting no incremental cache was available.
New loop process — Build artifact caching for staging: The 1m 51s Next.js build now dominates the...
items.decision_reason: The archived observation is still specific, no later queue-backed follow-up exists, and the repo does not show a staging-specific caching follow-on that closes the idea.
items.manual_judgment_notes: Carry forward as fresh fact-find work because the archived evidence is old and the current operational value of staging caching must be revalidated against today's deployment path.

items.title: Chunk count monitoring for Brikette staging
source_titles: AI-to-mechanistic — Chunk count monitoring: The ~13k _next/static files dominate the upload regardless of language filter. A deterministic script checking chunk count against a threshold could flag regressions. Trigger observation: CF upload time is bounded from below by the cost of hashing ~13k files locally (~30-40s), not by network transfer.
AI-to-mechanistic — Chunk count monitoring: The ~13k _next/static files dominate the upload regar...
items.decision_reason: The repo still shows no queue-backed monitoring follow-on for chunk explosion, and the archived signal remains a concrete mechanization candidate rather than a vague aspiration.
items.manual_judgment_notes: Carry forward as fresh fact-find work because the archived threshold and operational relevance need to be re-established before any monitoring gate is specified.

items.title: Divergence detection for app-nav dual sources
source_titles: AI-to-mechanistic — divergence detection between multiple hardcoded arrays could be automated by a script that compares route sets across the app; now moot since divergences are eliminated, but the pattern of "multiple files containing equivalent data" could trigger a lint rule
items.decision_reason: The archived result-review row itself records the issue as moot once the divergent arrays were eliminated.
items.manual_judgment_notes: Deterministic do-not-carry: the source artifact explicitly states the motivating divergence no longer exists.

items.title: Mechanize contrast pre-verification before token edits
source_titles: AI-to-mechanistic — The contrast pre-verification step (running pnpm tokens:contrast:check with proposed values before committing) could be a mechanistic pre-build check rather than a manual task step. Trigger observation: TASK-01 Red step required manual verification; this could be automated as a pre-token-edit gate. Signal is weak (single occurrence); not actionable yet.
items.decision_reason: Later guardrail work already mechanized the main contrast-check step through `scripts/validate-changes.sh` and `pnpm tokens:contrast:check`.
items.manual_judgment_notes: Deterministic do-not-carry: the motivating guardrail now exists in current repo policy and validation code.
